book.title: Pixel in Japan
book.subtitle: i videogiochi che hanno fatto la storia
book.author: Francesco Verni

system_principle: The system operates as a layered cultural graph in which 80 Japanese videogames (1979–2024) function as perceptual operators. These operators do not represent history but activate overlapping cultural fields that remain simultaneously active across time.

primary_loops.label: arcade_origin_field
primary_loops.propagation_logic: the arcade is not a historical phase but a foundational perceptual structure that remains active in all later forms of play
primary_loops.description: The arcade establishes the basic grammar of interaction, failure, repetition, and public visibility that persists across all subsequent eras.

primary_loops.label: jrpg_expansion_loop
primary_loops.propagation_logic: JRPG systems convert videogame interaction into extended narrative architectures (Dragon Quest, Final Fantasy as structural prototypes)
primary_loops.description: Narrative length becomes a cultural technology: time itself is reconfigured as playable structure.

primary_loops.label: horror_perceptual_loop
primary_loops.propagation_logic: Survival horror generates perception through absence, ambiguity, and spatial limitation (Silent Hill as canonical model)
primary_loops.description: Horror is produced not by content but by the structuring of visibility and uncertainty.

primary_loops.label: global_transmission_loop
primary_loops.propagation_logic: Japanese videogames operate as vectors of cultural encoding that are absorbed without explicit recognition by global players
primary_loops.description: Cultural transfer occurs at the level of mechanics, affect, and repetition rather than explicit representation.

primary_loops.label: nostalgic_stratification_loop
primary_loops.propagation_logic: nostalgia functions as an active structural layer rather than emotional residue
primary_loops.description: Earlier eras remain operational within contemporary play as embedded perceptual strata.

global_state.system_behavior: The 80 Japanese videogames form a distributed interpretive system: the reader does not reconstruct history but learns to navigate overlapping cultural fields.

catalog_connection.la_medusa_e_la_scimmia: shared authorial system within Peacock ontology
catalog_connection.la_singolarita_giappone: parallel philosophical encoding of Japanese cultural fields
catalog_connection.assolutamente_giappone: adjacent cultural mapping of Japan across media systems
